\noindent The total sample size required to obtain an
estimate of the mean monthly payroll with a
precision of 300,000 at 95\% confidence is 424.
This is more than 10\% of the population size;
therefore, we were right to use the
finite-population correction factor. If we had
not anticipated that the required sample size
would have been unnecessarily large, not taking
the finite-population correction factor into
account would have resulted in a sample size of
524.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
